features.summary: Chevron reported Q1 2021 adjusted earnings of $1.7 billion ($0.90 per share), marking their best financial performance in a year as the global economy recovers. Free cash flow excluding working capital reached $3.4 billion, significantly higher than last year. The company increased its dividend by 4% ($0.05 per share), extending its streak to 34 consecutive years of dividend increases. CapEx was down over 40% year-over-year at $3.4 billion, with net debt ratio at 22.5%. Winter Storm Uri impacted earnings by approximately $300 million after tax.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued strong financial performance as the global economy recovers from COVID-19, though they remain cautious about sustained recovery given ongoing virus challenges in many countries. They anticipate Q2 production to be reduced by 90,000 boe/d due to planned turnarounds, primarily at Gorgon Train 3. The company maintains 2021 production guidance of 0% to 3% growth and organic CapEx guidance of $14 billion. Management expressed confidence in delivering stronger financial performance while advancing lower carbon initiatives, with free cash flow expected to grow 10% annually over five years.
features.risks: Key risks include ongoing COVID-19 impacts on global economic recovery, with many countries still struggling with virus control. OPEC+ production curtailments continue to affect market dynamics, with an estimated 40,000 boe/d impact primarily in Kazakhstan. The Tengiz project faces schedule pressure due to pandemic-related workforce challenges, though cost pressures may be offset by efficiencies and foreign exchange benefits. Potential regulatory changes in California regarding hydraulic fracturing and internal combustion engines could impact operations, though management indicated minimal material impact to current operations.
features.key_drivers: Higher oil prices drove upstream earnings up $1.4 billion quarter-over-quarter, while downstream margins and timing effects also contributed positively. The Noble acquisition integration is proceeding well with synergies doubled from $300 million to $600 million, achieving 80% of targets. Tengiz workforce remobilization reached 25,000 (near the 26,000 Q1 target) with all 86 modules now on foundations. The company is advancing lower carbon initiatives including hydrogen partnerships with Toyota, carbon capture projects, and renewable energy investments, positioning for future growth in these sectors.
